# Fresco: Frequency-domain scan context for lidar-based place recognition with translation and rotation invariance:

## Accepted structured facts

- **datasets:** MulRan KAIST03, KITTI 08, Oxford Radar RobotCar 2019-01-11, 2019-01-13, 2019-01-24 and 2019-01-51 — `source_fact`; manual, p. 5–6, § Abstract;III. FreSCo;IV. Experiments, Fig. 2-9;Table I-III
- **input_unit:** single ground-removed LiDAR scan, compact flattened 2-D point clouds are retained for pose estimation after retrieval — `source_fact`; manual, p. 3–5, § Abstract;III. FreSCo;IV. Experiments, Fig. 2-9;Table I-III
- **invariances:** planar translation from the Fourier-magnitude theorem, limited in practice by finite sampling, cropping and occlusion, yaw rotation after circular-shift minimization — `researcher_judgement`; manual, p. 3–5, § Abstract;III. FreSCo;IV. Experiments, Fig. 2-9;Table I-III
- **representation:** ground-removed max-height Cartesian BEV, 2-D Fourier magnitude, cropped low-frequency polar FreSCo descriptor, KD-tree key retrieval, circular-shift matching, two-stage planar NICP followed by optional 3-D ICP pose estimation — `source_fact`; manual, p. 3–5, § Abstract;III. FreSCo;IV. Experiments, Fig. 2-9;Table I-III
- **uses_learning:** no — `researcher_judgement`; manual, p. 1–7, § Abstract;III. FreSCo;IV. Experiments, Fig. 2-9;Table I-III
- **uses_sequence:** no — `researcher_judgement`; manual, p. 1–7, § Abstract;III. FreSCo;IV. Experiments, Fig. 2-9;Table I-III
